[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] '''Heget''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A capable [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and instructor, Heget was a loyal devotee of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. However, he became disillusioned with the constant infighting and [[Politics/Legends|political]] backstabbing that permeated the Empire and sought to remove himself from it by transferring to the remote [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] under [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. Sarne, however, declared himself a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Palpatine at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and fostered a personality cult amongst his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]], in which loyalty to him was rewarded over loyalty to the Empire. Heget refused to play Sarne's games and was rewarded by being posted to command [[Imperial Depot 4290-1]] on the backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shintel]]. There, he trained his allotted personnel into a fighting unit.

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|evicted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Falling back to Shintel, Sarne took everything of value from the depot and stranded Heget and his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] on the planet by destroying the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] on all the base's [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. Shortly thereafter, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of Sarne. Heget offered its crew a deal to help him contact Imperial authorities in exchange for information on Sarne's possible destinations.

==Biography==
===Early service===
Heget was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Dedicated to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] vision of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], Heget rose to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Major/Legends|major]] and commanded one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Army/Legends|Army's]] most highly decorated [[special missions battalion]]s. However, he grew weary of the backstabbing and [[Politics/Legends|political]] maneuvering that pervaded Imperial culture. After two opposing [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] clashed trying to secure his loyalty, Heget requested a transfer to the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. He believed that an isolated backwater [[Sector/Legends|sector]] would free him from petty political squabbles. A young [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] under Heget's command, [[Danthe]], accompanied him to his new posting.<ref name="SD" />

===Kathol sector and command on Shintel===
Heget found himself under the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of Moff [[Kentor Sarne]], an ambitious man who cultivated a personality clique amongst the [[Moff Sarne's forces|personnel]] under his command and rewarded loyalty to himself over loyalty to the Empire.<ref name="SD" /> Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], which resulted in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine, Sarne declared himself a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and withdrew the Kathol sector from the Empire.<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Heget, now a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]], refused to play by Sarne's rules, leading to his assignment to command [[Imperial Depot 4290-1]] on the backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shintel]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. For two [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he trained the personnel under his command, whipping them into prime fighting condition. The [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] became specialized in wilderness survival, special recon, and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] [[Ground warfare/Legends|combat]] operations. Under Heget's command, the base personnel developed into a tight-knit community.<ref name="SD" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]].<ref name="TSB" /> Retreating with his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], Sarne began stopping at his supply depots throughout the sector. Arriving at Shintel, Sarne uploaded the depot's arsenal supplies and transferred to the fleet those personnel at the base who were loyal to him. Conversely, he rotated those personnel from his fleet who were not loyal to him personally to the depot. Sarne also ordered his forces to destroy the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] on any [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]-capable [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] stationed at the base, as well as the [[Courier droid/Legends|courier droids]]. He then placed [[Mark III Merr-Sonn Defender Ion Mine|ion mines]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the planet. To Sarne's mind, the personnel on Shintel were traitors and deserved their fate. However, he did not want to risk unrest in his ranks by openly killing the base personnel, and he understood that he might need the depot during his eventual campaign to retake the sector. To that end, Sarne left Heget with orders to hold the depot and construct a landing field suitable for [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray Blastboats]].<ref name="SD" />

===Dealing with the New Republic===
[[File:FarStar-Shintel.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Heget greets the command crew of the ''FarStar'' on Shintel.]]
Heget saw Sarne's actions as the betrayal they were, and the introduction of new personnel to the base caused fault lines to appear in the camp. The personnel split into two factions. Heget and his allies wanted to disregard Sarne's orders, seeing them as a waste of resources, and concentrate on restoring communications to [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial command authority]]. A faction led by the Major Danthe—who was the outpost's second-in-command—believed that Sarne would return and that his extreme measures were meant to preserve the security of the base. A [[Standard week/Legends|week]] after Sarne's visit, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived at Shintel in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of the Moff. Heget, seeing a possibility of rescue, invited the New Republic [[Corvette/Legends|ship]] to land on the planet. The ''FarStar'' cleared a path through the ion [[Mine/Legends|mines]] and landed at the depot. Heget greeted the command crew awkwardly alongside Danthe, and then invited them to the [[Officer/Legends|officer's]] [[Barracks/Legends|barracks]], where he offered the ''FarStar''{{'s}} commander, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], a deal. He would supply the New Republic with information regarding Sarne's possible destinations. In return, the ''FarStar'' would transport him and an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] to the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] so that he could contact Imperial authorities and inform them of the situation on Shintel. He also offered to resupply the ''FarStar'' with provisions from the base's stores. Although wary of Heget, Adrimetrum agreed to the deal.<ref name="SD" />

{{RPG|"[[Shintel Downtime]]"}}
As the ''FarStar'' prepared to depart Shintel, Adrimetrum was approached by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Palme]], an officer on the base and secretly a deep-cover [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for Sarne. He claimed that Heget was going to betray Adrimetrum, and offered to provide her with a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] containing a copy of Sarne's navigational charts for the sector. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], four [[Agent/Legends|agents]] under his command infiltrated the ''FarStar'' and attempted to steal a [[Hyperdrive motivator/Legends|hyperdrive motivator]] from one of the corvette's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]]. Palme's plan was twofold; the datacard, while seemingly genuine, contained [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] that would activate a booby-trap hardwired into the ''FarStar''. Before its service to the New Republic, the ''FarStar'' had been part of Sarne's forces, and the Moff had installed failsafe programming in the ship in case of emergency situations. The attempted theft of the hyperdrive motivator was never meant to succeed, as Palme wanted to cast doubt on Heget in an attempt to stop him from leaving Shintel.<ref name="SD" />

Adrimetrum uploaded the information from the datacard into the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Computer/Legends|computers]], but the attempted sabotage by Sarne's men did not change her decision regarding the Colonel. Heget departed Shintel aboard the ''FarStar'' as planned, but, as the corvette engaged its hyperdrive, the booby-trap activated, causing a massive explosion that destroyed the hyperdrive [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. As the corvette recovered from the sabotage, the ''[[Steel Fist]]'', a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] belonging to the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] group [[Qulok's Fist]], decanted from hyperspace and [[Skirmish at Shintel|attacked]] the ''FarStar''. The booby-trap activated aboard the ''FarStar'' was but one stage of Sarne's plans to harass the New Republic corvette; Sarne had contracted Qulok's Fist to follow the ''FarStar'' and attack them in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]] once disabled. The New Republic vessel rallied from the initial surprise and repulsed the attack. Heget was not pleased with the turn of the events and was indignant if anyone attempted to cast suspicion on him for the sabotage. The ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], [[Lofryyhn]], jury-rigged the ''FarStar''{{'s}} engines with parts cannibalized from the corvette's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] complement, allowing them to reach ''Tanquilla Beach'' for repairs.<ref name="SD" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
By 8 ABY, Heget was middle-aged but in excellent health. His face was lined, and he had tired [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], but he was mentally sharp and alert. A staunch supporter of Palpatine's New Order, he grew disillusioned with the constant political maneuvering and attempted to remove himself from the games of ambitious Moffs. Heget had no use for politics, a distaste that effectively stalled his career in the Kathol sector when he refused to pander to Moff Sarne's personality cult. Sarne's reaction—to post a decorated veteran officer to a remote supply post—showed just how much the Moff prized loyalty over ability. An honorable man, Heget was loyal to his [[Troop/Legends|troops]]<ref name="SD" /> and inspired the personnel under his command to return his loyalty.<ref name="CSWE" /> Despite his allegiance to the Empire, Heget had no problem working with New Republic personnel to achieve his goal of contacting Imperial authorities to inform them of the plight in which Sarne had left the personnel stationed on Shintel.<ref name="SD" />

A military man, Heget was trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], with a specialty in [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistols]]. He was also trained to use [[blaster artillery]] and vehicle-mounted weapons, and he was skilled in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed]] and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]]. Heget was able to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] ground and [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|hover vehicles]], and he was skilled in [[Walker/Legends|walker]] operations, with a specialization in piloting AT-STs. An able commander and combat trainer, Heget was knowledgeable about enforcing Imperial law. Heget was trained to repair blasters, and he had survival and first-aid training. He carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="SD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Heget first appeared in ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', the fourth [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure<ref name="SD" /> featured in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]].<ref name="TDC">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref>

In ''Shintel Downtime'', the players have the option of refusing Heget's deal and leaving him behind on Shintel either through their own choices or by believing Lieutenant Palme's assertions that Heget will betray them. As the adventure continues under the assumption that the players help Heget, while also taking advantage of Palme's datacard, this article assumes that the ''FarStar'' chose to help Heget but did not believe Palme's claims about Heget's deceitfulness.<ref name="SD" />

If Heget is aboard the ''FarStar'' during the battle with the ''Steel Fist'', the gamemaster has the option to have Heget call the base's remaining [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] into combat if the ''FarStar'' is having trouble dealing with their opponents. If Heget is informed of Palme's attempts to subvert his deal with the New Republic crew, he will [[Prisoner/Legends|incarcerate]] Palme in the [[Prison/Legends|brig]] for his actions.<ref name="SD" />

==Appearances==
